target.name: ideaForge
target.founder: Ankit Mehta, Ashish Bhatt, Rahul Singh

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Joby Aviation
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Public), post-money $6.86B

private.name: Archer Aviation
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Public), post-money $3.33B

data.narrative:
ideaForge is a public-stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector that has raised $50M+. The relevant peer universe consists of seven private companies in the same sector, including Joby Aviation, Archer Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, and Elroy Air. No public comparables exist in this narrowly defined space.

Peer-median valuation analysis was not applicable due to insufficient comparable datapoints meeting standard filtering criteria. The sector contains only five total peers, and application of stage-matching, adjacent-stage, and valuation-tier filters yielded fewer than three datapoints in any single cohort—below the minimum threshold for robust median estimation. An all-sector median approach was rejected as methodologically misleading given the heterogeneity of applications within Drones & Autonomous (urban air mobility, package delivery, industrial inspection).

The absence of public comparables and constrained private peer set limits triangulation to single-method approaches. Any valuation conclusion would rest heavily on transaction precedent or discounted cash flow analysis applied to ideaForge's specific unit economics and market opportunity—neither of which is present in the available data. Key uncertainties include the company's revenue trajectory, path to profitability, and how its technology and market position compare quantitatively to the identified private peer set. Establishing valuation requires either expansion of the comparable universe or reliance on fundamental cash flow modeling with disclosed financial performance.
